'''Luke Dyer''' (born {{Birth date|1981|8|15|df=y}}) is a former [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. Dyer had played at representative level for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level for the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League]], the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], the (Celtic) [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]], and the [[Central Comets]] in the [[Queensland Cup]], as a {{rlp|wg}}, or {{rlp|ce}}.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Dyer was born in [[Penrith, New South Wales|Penrith, New South Wales, Australia]].

==Playing career==
Dyer played for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the 2004 [[Rugby League World Sevens]] pre-season tournament.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Only Melbourne|title=Storm hoping lucky sevens will reveal next young gun|url=http://www.onlymelbourne.com.au/melbourne_details.php?id=3879
|accessdate=2007-10-24}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Sevens tournament made for speedsters |url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/rugby-league-sevens-tournament-made-for-speedsters/EDMBUWD75QWFJ2JROIIRJNEVYI/ |website=The New Zealand Herald |access-date=24 October 2021}}</ref> He was later released by Melbourne, returning to [[Penrith Panthers]] during the [[2004 NRL season]], making his NRL debut for Penrith against Melbourne in round 17.

Dyer represented for Wales in [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|Rugby League World Cup]] qualifying matches.<ref>{{cite news|author=Andy Wilson|newspaper=The Guardian|title=McCormack celebrates as Scotland make it to World Cup|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/nov/05/rugbyleague.sport|access-date=2008-04-19|date=2007-11-05|location=London}}</ref>

Dyer scored the Crusaders first and only try in their first game in the Super League.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7866936.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Leeds 28-6 Celtic Crusaders|date=2009-02-06|access-date=2010-05-11}}</ref>
